title: Implementing a RESTful API for a Simple Rate-Limiting System
statement:
Design a RESTful API to manage a rate-limiting system. The system has two types of resources: users and plans. Users can have plans associated with them, and each plan has a specific rate limit (e.g., 10 requests per minute). Implement the following endpoints:

* POST /api/users: Creates a new user with a rate limit (default: 10 requests per minute). The request body should contain the user's email, name, and rate limit.
* GET /api/users/:id/plans: Retrieves a list of plans associated with the specified user.
* POST /api/plans: Creates a new plan with a specified rate limit (default: 10 requests per minute). The request body should contain the plan's name and rate limit.
* PUT /api/plans/:id: Updates an existing plan's rate limit.
* DELETE /api/users/:id: Deletes a user and their associated plans.

Implement the following constraints:

* The rate limit is enforced using a simple token bucket algorithm.
* The system should handle concurrent requests and rate limiting accurately.
* The API should return a JSON response with a `success` property indicating whether the operation was successful.

You can use an in-memory array to store users and plans. Make sure to handle errors and edge cases properly.

**Visible Test Cases**

* `v_tc1`: Create a new user with a custom rate limit (e.g., 20 requests per minute).
* `v_tc2`: Retrieve a list of plans associated with a user.
* `h_tc3`: Attempt to create a new user with an invalid rate limit (e.g., -1).
* `h_tc4`: Attempt to update a plan with an invalid rate limit (e.g., -1).
* `h_tc5`: Delete a user with an associated plan.

**Hidden Test Cases**

* `h_tc6`: Create multiple users with concurrent requests and verify that the rate limit is enforced correctly.
* `h_tc7`: Update the rate limit of a plan and verify that the change is reflected correctly.

Note: You can use the provided starter code as a starting point. Make sure to implement the required endpoints and constraints accurately.
constraints: Use only the in-memory array provided to store users and plans.
Implement a simple token bucket algorithm to enforce rate limiting.

endpoints.description: Creates a new user with a rate limit. Request body: email, name, rate limit.
Retrieves a list of plans associated with the specified user.
Creates a new plan with a specified rate limit. Request body: name, rate limit.
Updates an existing plan's rate limit.
Deletes a user and their associated plans.
